assertions.reason: The user-visible typecheck test surface includes four regression cases that exercise trait impl receiver-kind mismatches and they all pass under the real test executable.
evidence: test/test_typecheck.ml defines negative receiver-kind cases expecting `receiver mismatch` for `trait_recv_ref_vs_value`, `trait_recv_ref_vs_mutref`, `trait_recv_mutref_vs_ref`, and `trait_recv_value_vs_ref`.
nix develop -c dune exec ./test/test_typecheck.exe -- test negative 21
nix develop -c dune exec ./test/test_typecheck.exe -- test negative 22
nix develop -c dune exec ./test/test_typecheck.exe -- test negative 23
nix develop -c dune exec ./test/test_typecheck.exe -- test negative 24

frictions: `dune runtest --no-buffer test/test_typecheck.exe` does not match any known test target, so validation used repo-wide `dune runtest` plus direct `dune exec ./test/test_typecheck.exe` and focused Alcotest indices.
summary: Receiver-kind regression coverage is present and passing from the real CLI/compiler test surface. Matching receiver controls also pass in the same executable output.
